verdict_reasoning: EREG (epiregulin) is a secreted EGF-family ligand. It is synthesized as a transmembrane precursor (pro-epiregulin) that is shed by ADAM proteases (primarily ADAM10/17) to release the soluble mature form. The pro-form transiently resides on the plasma membrane but is rapidly cleaved and shed; it does not stably persist at the cell surface. The mature secreted form binds EGFR/ErbB4 on target cells in a reversible non-covalent manner and would wash off. No evidence of stable surface retention via GPI anchor, stable complex partner, or other wash-resistant mechanism. Primary biology is as a paracrine/autocrine secreted growth factor.
